title: Disaster Risk Communication: Plan: Goals and objectives
content: Based on insights gained through the UNDERSTAND process, risk communicators need to define a specific goal you intend to achieve through disaster risk communication
snippet: Based on insights gained through the UNDERSTAND process, risk communicators need to define a specific goal you intend to achieve through disaster risk communication

title: Disaster Risk Communication Hub: Understand: Consider what influences the issue
content: Effective disaster risk communication will focus on a clearly defined issue.
snippet: Effective disaster risk communication will focus on a clearly defined issue.

title: How will tropical cyclones impact coastal critical infrastructure — including nuclear reactors — in the future?
content: Powerful cyclones can push seawater miles inland, threatening densely populated communities and critical infrastructure built along coastal areas.
snippet: Powerful cyclones can push seawater miles inland, threatening densely populated communities and critical infrastructure built along coastal areas.

title: AI for social risk forecasting and explanation: The power of machine learning–based social risk models
content: The report demonstrates how these models capture dynamic interactions between climate stressors, economic conditions, and social perceptions, strengthening risk identification and early warning capacities.
snippet: The report demonstrates how these models capture dynamic interactions between climate stressors, economic conditions, and social perceptions, strengthening risk identification and early warning capaci

title: A ‘super typhoon’ just devastated the Mariana Islands — months before peak storm season
content: The storm exposes the U.S. commonwealth's climate risks, economic fragility, and federal strain.
snippet: The storm exposes the U.S. commonwealth's climate risks, economic fragility, and federal strain.

title: Podcast: Natural disasters are not natural
content: Are disasters purely natural, or do human actions make them worse? In this episode of Beyond the Brink, we bust the myth that natural disasters are purely acts of nature.
snippet: Are disasters purely natural, or do human actions make them worse? In this episode of Beyond the Brink, we bust the myth that natural disasters are purely acts of nature.

title: Reducing flood risk in a changing climate: lessons from Türkiye’s integrated approach
content: River rehabilitation works carried out through institutional cooperation in Türkiye constitute a concrete example of DRR–CCA integration in practice
snippet: River rehabilitation works carried out through institutional cooperation in Türkiye constitute a concrete example of DRR–CCA integration in practice

title: ScienceAlert
content: ScienceAlert publishes timely, trusted science news to enlighten and entertain millions of readers each month. They are an independently run online news source, and journalists cover the important scientific issues of the time
snippet: ScienceAlert publishes timely, trusted science news to enlighten and entertain millions of readers each month. They are an independently run online news source, and journalists cover the important sci

title: The April 2024 Hualien earthquake: Preparedness for earthquakes is key
content: Despite the impact of the earthquake on people & on the physical environment of the local area, it could have been a lot worse were it not for the policies & measures that have been pursued in Taiwan over many years to reduce vulnerability to earthquakes

title: Disaster risk financing term sheet: Debt pause clauses
content: This Term Sheet covers the key terms and conditions of debt pause clauses available from six Multilateral Development Banks and three bilateral creditors.
snippet: This Term Sheet covers the key terms and conditions of debt pause clauses available from six Multilateral Development Banks and three bilateral creditors.

title: A framework for stress-testing investment portfolios against nature-related shocks
content: Joel Johannes-Gold, Nicola Ranger and Anne Marie Eikeset present a new framework designed to estimate the financial impact of nature-related shocks on individual companies, and the results from a pilot study.
snippet: Joel Johannes-Gold, Nicola Ranger and Anne Marie Eikeset present a new framework designed to estimate the financial impact of nature-related shocks on individual companies, and the results from a pilo

title: On the Seattle fault, the biggest quakes aren’t the most likely
content: Smaller quakes from secondary faults—which are not included in national seismic hazard modeling—occur more frequently than previously thought.
snippet: Smaller quakes from secondary faults—which are not included in national seismic hazard modeling—occur more frequently than previously thought.

title: Before the wave arrives: disaster risk reduction and tsunami events
content: Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) strategies—especially early warning systems, public awareness, and evacuation drills—significantly reduce fatalities during tsunami events by enabling timely and effective evacuation.
snippet: Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) strategies—especially early warning systems, public awareness, and evacuation drills—significantly reduce fatalities during tsunami events by enabling timely and effectiv

title: Sea level rise maps reveal: coastal cities facing climate flooding, acidification, and erosion now
content: Sea level rise driven by climate change is increasingly threatening coastal cities worldwide through flooding, ocean acidification, and erosion, highlighting the urgent need for adaptation and resilience strategies.
snippet: Sea level rise driven by climate change is increasingly threatening coastal cities worldwide through flooding, ocean acidification, and erosion, highlighting the urgent need for adaptation and resilie

title: Our changing climate in 2025
content: This report provides an overview of how the climate is changing globally and in specific contexts (like Ireland), combining recent scientific evidence, observed trends, and projections.
snippet: This report provides an overview of how the climate is changing globally and in specific contexts (like Ireland), combining recent scientific evidence, observed trends, and projections.

title: Disaster displacement risk in Kenya: An overview of the risk of future displacement
content: This report and accompanying short risk profile are designed to help governments and their partners to reduce risk, strengthen resilience and minimise the impacts of disasters, including internal displacement.
snippet: This report and accompanying short risk profile are designed to help governments and their partners to reduce risk, strengthen resilience and minimise the impacts of disasters, including internal disp

title: High impact low probability events: research landscape and future opportunities
content: The review synthesizes current academic discourse on HILPs to identify their defining characteristics and implications for disaster preparedness and response.They analyse a dataset of 109 papers, highlighting the limitations of conventional planning tools
snippet: The review synthesizes current academic discourse on HILPs to identify their defining characteristics and implications for disaster preparedness and response.They analyse a dataset of 109 papers, high

title: Assessing agricultural drought risk under CMIP6 scenarios using hybrid AI models and satellite-derived TVDI
content: This study focuses on predicting the temperature vegetation dryness index (TVDI), an agricultural drought index, for a Mango orchard in Tamale, Ghana, using climate projections and satellite imagery.
snippet: This study focuses on predicting the temperature vegetation dryness index (TVDI), an agricultural drought index, for a Mango orchard in Tamale, Ghana, using climate projections and satellite imagery.

title: Basin-scale inventory and exposure assessment of hanging glaciers, Central Himalaya
content: The report identifies 219 hanging glaciers covering 71.7 km², with an estimated ice volume of 2.39 km³, and introduces a classification framework based on morphology and slope characteristics.
snippet: The report identifies 219 hanging glaciers covering 71.7 km², with an estimated ice volume of 2.39 km³, and introduces a classification framework based on morphology and slope characteristics.

title: From flames to fungi: Prescribed burns and soil restoration
content: Plant science researchers and the UConn Fire Department are using prescribed burns to mitigate brush fires and study the role of microbes in soil recovery to generate new insights to help Connecticut manage rising wildfire risk
snippet: Plant science researchers and the UConn Fire Department are using prescribed burns to mitigate brush fires and study the role of microbes in soil recovery to generate new insights to help Connecticut

title: Relocating Venice among the options explored to protect city against sea-level rise
content: Relocating the city of Venice is among four potential options – including movable barriers, ring dikes and closing the Venetian Lagoon - that could help it adapt to future sea-level rise over the next 200 years, according a new study .
snippet: Relocating the city of Venice is among four potential options – including movable barriers, ring dikes and closing the Venetian Lagoon - that could help it adapt to future sea-level rise over the next

title: Impact of sea level rise on land loss in Vietnam
content: This report presents insights how sea level rise may affect land availability across economic sectors in Vietnam.
snippet: This report presents insights how sea level rise may affect land availability across economic sectors in Vietnam.

title: Earthquake-resisting building in Pakistan
content: This case study highlights that the post-1935 earthquake buildings performed well in Pakistan during the 1955 earthquake.
snippet: This case study highlights that the post-1935 earthquake buildings performed well in Pakistan during the 1955 earthquake.

title: Nebraska Arbor Day 2024 tornado
content: This case study explains how the work of the National Weather Service (NWS) Omaha-Valley office was instrumental in reducing the negative human health impacts of the Arbor Day tornado outbreak.
snippet: This case study explains how the work of the National Weather Service (NWS) Omaha-Valley office was instrumental in reducing the negative human health impacts of the Arbor Day tornado outbreak.

title: Drought, but not famine: 1991-1993
content: This case study highlights how the Southern Africa drought response of 1992-1993 demonstrated how coordinated and timely action was effectively able to avert disastrous famine consequences – even at regional scale.
snippet: This case study highlights how the Southern Africa drought response of 1992-1993 demonstrated how coordinated and timely action was effectively able to avert disastrous famine consequences – even at r

title: Transboundary haze in Southeast Asia
content: This case study explores how transboundary haze in Southeast Asia has become a recurring intermittent problem in the region in recent decades.
snippet: This case study explores how transboundary haze in Southeast Asia has become a recurring intermittent problem in the region in recent decades.

title: The National Briefing on Societal Resilience - Issue 57
content: The latest National Briefing on Societal Resilience on behalf of the National Consortium for Societal Resilience (NCSR+) provides some highlights from the recent 4th National Conference on Societal Resilience held at The University of Manchester.

title: Wildland fire smoke and birth defects in California
content: This study investigates the association between wildfire smoke exposure, specifically fine particulate matter (PM2.5), and the risk of selected structural birth defects in the San Joaquin Valley of California.

title: Journal of Exposure Science & Environmental Epidemiology (JESEE)

title: Waikīkī faces escalating threat of sewage-contaminated flooding as sea level rises
content: A study reveals that Waikīkī in Hawai‘i is facing a fundamental shift in flood hazards as sea levels rise–transitioning from a flooding that is driven primarily by rainfall to events increasingly dominated by tidal processes.

title: Metric identifies at-risk mangroves before they disappear
content: The tool flagged vulnerable mangrove patches a decade in advance, offering a path toward preventive conservation.
snippet: The tool flagged vulnerable mangrove patches a decade in advance, offering a path toward preventive conservation.

title: Pacific Prepared Podcast: It’s hoped that a new type of early warning system for Tonga will help people to be more prepared for disasters
content: An early warning system is being rolled out by the Tongan government and they’re hoping it will change the way people are able to respond to disasters.
snippet: An early warning system is being rolled out by the Tongan government and they’re hoping it will change the way people are able to respond to disasters.

title: Systemic risk is the hidden tax on growth. Here's how insurance can help build economic resilience
content: In a period of unprecedented risk complexity, the insurance industry can help transform systemic uncertainty into a foundation for sustainable growth.
snippet: In a period of unprecedented risk complexity, the insurance industry can help transform systemic uncertainty into a foundation for sustainable growth.

title: Οpen-source Python-based software boosts space-weather modelling
content: Free, community-friendly tool makes it easier for scientists to model how electromagnetic waves affect high-energy particles in space.
snippet: Free, community-friendly tool makes it easier for scientists to model how electromagnetic waves affect high-energy particles in space.

title: Building disaster resilience in Barbados' tourism sector: Lessons from Atlantis Submarines
content: In the tourism sector, where safety, reliability, and reputation are inseparable, Atlantis Submarines shows resilience isn’t a one-off task or static plan but a living system shaped by people, processes, and continuous learning.
